frameworks.name: Bayesian models of behavior and neural data
frameworks.one_line: Family of models formalizing how uncertainty shapes behavior and neural computation

frameworks.name: graphical models
frameworks.one_line: Used as a unifying framework to compare assumptions across Bayesian approaches

frameworks.name: neural representation of uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: Reviewed theories of how brains encode probability distributions

core_question: How do different Bayesian models of behavior and neural data relate to one another under shared assumptions about the world?
key_assumptions: Uncertainty from unreliable or incomplete information affects behavior
Behavior can be characterized as minimizing the effect of uncertainty
Different Bayesian models derive from different assumptions about the world
Graphical models can expose structural commonalities across approaches
key_findings: Bayesian models of behavior and neural data form a diverse family tied to differing world assumptions
Graphical-model analysis reveals commonalities and differences across these Bayesian approaches
Multiple candidate theories exist for how the brain itself represents uncertainty
interpretations: A graphical-model lens clarifies how disparate Bayesian behavioral and neural models relate to one another
Uncertainty representation is a unifying problem that organizes the space of Bayesian theories of the brain
